properties.remark: PUBLIC REPORTED A LARGE DUST STORM MOVING WEST NEAR INTERSTATE 80 VIA VIDEO ON SOCIAL MEDIA.
1 INCH HAIL REPORTED 1 MILE SOUTH OF LAKE TAHOE AIRPORT.
REPORTS OF WATER OVER 1 FOOT DEEP IN #2 LANE ON I-80 W NEAR FLORISTON.
NV ENERGY MESONET SENSOR REPORTED 59 MPH THUNDERSTORM OUTFLOW WIND GUST.
PUBLIC REPORTED BLOWING DUST WITH REDUCED VISIBILITIES TO 1/2 MILE, BUT PREVAILING 2 MILES AND WIND GUSTS UP 50 MPH.
KRNV REPORTED 1 INCH SIZED HAIL WITH PICTURES.
NDOT MESONET SENSOR REPORTED 69 MPH WIND GUST WITH SUSTAINED WINDS OF 50 MPH.
LOVELOCK DERBY ASOS REPORTED A THUNDERSTORM OUTFLOW WIND GUST OF 60 MPH WITH A SUSTAINED WIND OF 45 MPH.
GOLF BALL SIZED HAIL AT EBBETTS PASS PER VIDEO POSTED TO NWS RENO FACEBOOK PAGE.
DUST LIMITED VISIBILITY ALONG INTERSTATE 80 FOR SEVERAL HOURS. DUST REDUCED VISIBILITY DOWN TO LESS THAN 0.5 MILE AT TIMES FOR LOVELOCK.
83 MPH WIND GUST REPORTED AT BARREL SPRINGS RAWS IN NORTHERN WASHOE COUNTY, NEVADA.

NWS RENO (REV) STORM SURVEY. A MESOANTICYCLONE EMBEDDED WITHIN A DEEP PYROCUMULONIMBUS UPDRAFT INSIDE THE LOYALTON WILDFIRE PERIMETER GENERATED AN EF1 RATED ANTICYCLONIC TORNADO THAT SNAPPED (DI 28, DOD 4) AND UPROOTED (DI 28, DOD 3) SEVERAL JEFFREY PINE TREES ALONG A 0.43-MILE PATH WITHIN ROBERTS CANYON. THE PYRO-GENETIC TORNADO SPUN UP 0.15 MILES NORTH-NORTHEAST OF THE INTERSECTION OF FOREST SERVICE ROAD 182 AND FOREST SERVICE ROAD 31182 AND DISSIPATED 0.44 MILES EAST OF THE AFOREMENTIONED INTERSECTION. THE DAMAGE PATH HAD A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 137 YARDS. THE ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS WERE 104 MPH. NO INJURIES OR DEATHS OCCURRED WITH THIS PYRO-GENETIC TORNADO.

NWS RENO (REV) STORM SURVEY. A MESOANTICYCLONE EMBEDDED WITHIN A DEEP PYROCUMULONIMBUS UPDRAFT INSIDE THE LOYALTON WILDFIRE PERIMETER GENERATED AN EF1 RATED ANTICYCLONIC TORNADO THAT SNAPPED SEVERAL QUAKING ASPEN TREES (DI 27, DOD 4) ALONG A 0.09-MILE PATH ON THE NORTHEAST FACING SLOPE OF ROBERTS CANYON. THE PYRO-GENETIC TORNADO SPUN UP 0.37 MILES SOUTH-SOUTHWEST OF THE INTERSECTION OF FOREST SERVICE ROAD 182 AND FOREST SERVICE ROAD 31182 AND DISSIPATED 0.46 MILES SOUTH-SOUTHWEST OF THE AFOREMENTIONED INTERSECTION. THE DAMAGE PATH HAD A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 20 YARDS. THE ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS WERE 107 MPH. NO INJURIES OR DEATHS OCCURRED WITH THIS PYRO-GENETIC TORNADO.

BYSTANDERS ALONG CA 70 AND US 395 NEAR HALLELUJAH JUNCTION CAPTURED MULTIPLE VIDEOS AND PHOTOGRAPHS OF AN ANTICYCLONICALLY ROTATING TORNADO PENDANT TO THE BASE OF A PYROCUMULONIMBUS CLOUD. THE PYRO-GENETIC TORNADO CAUSED NO DAMAGE FROM WHICH THE NWS COULD ASSIGN AN EF-SCALE RATING AS IT TRAVERSED ACROSS AN AREA OF BURNED SAGEBRUSH. FROM PHOTOGRAPHS AND VIDEO EVIDENCE, THE APPROXIMATE PATH OF THE TORNADO WAS CALCULATED. THE TORNADO TRAVELED ALONG A 3.16-MILE PATH FROM THE HEAD OF ROBERTS CANYON TO 1.33 MILES SOUTHWEST OF HALLELUJAH JUNCTION. THE MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH WAS APPROXIMATELY 156 YARDS. NO INJURIES OR DEATHS OCCURRED WITH THIS TORNADO.
SEVERAL RESIDENTIAL STREETS FLOODED WITH OVER 6 INCHES OF WATER ON ROADS.
LYON SO REPORTED CLOSURE OF HIGHWAY 95A BETWEEN SILVER SPRINGS AND FERNLEY TO CLEAR WATER AND MUD FROM THE ROAD.
STREET FLOODING REPORTED IN SEVERAL AREAS OF FERNLEY. FLOOD WATER REACHED ONE HOME AND A FEW OTHER HOMES REPORTED FLOODED YARDS. MOST OF THE HEAVY RAIN FELL IN FERNLEY BETWEEN 840 AND 915 PM.
LYON COUNTY ROADS DEPARTMENT REPORTED SOME FLOOD DAMAGE TO LOCAL ROADS IN SILVER SPRINGS, MAINLY EAST OF US-95A. MOST RAIN FELL BETWEEN 8 AND 9 PM.
